title: Kibatalia merrittii
taxonomy: Kibatalia merrittii is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The genus Kibatalia belongs to the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which is a part of the Apocynaceae family. The plant is native to the Philippines, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Apocynaceae family. Further classification within the plant kingdom includes its order Gentianales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae. This comprehensive classification helps in understanding the plant's relationships with other organisms and its place in the broader plant kingdom.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and combat infections.
